Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for and with James Palma (Respondent).
Brian O'Grady appeared by telephone for Delia Palma (Petitioner).

Mr. Kithas stated that they have two glitches with taking of depositions. Both Counsel indicated that they are ready to go to trial. The First Amended Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Instruments, etc. is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on March 26, 2018 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court/a copy of the order and notice of trial was provided to Counsel).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian O'Grady appeared by telephone for Delia Palma-Hauser.
John Kithas for and with James Palma (Respondent).

Mr. Kithas stated that they have been trying to set depositions, that they had two set for June 29th, that one went forward, that they were unable to get the one with the Doctor done and that he wants to have it done by December 20th because he will be on vacation. Mr. O'Grady stated that there are two discovery matters remaining, one being the deposition of the Respondent and the other with the Doctor for which he is trying to schedule a date. Mr. Kithas stated that he does not believe trying mediation again makes sense and that it will be a seven to ten day trial. Mr. O'Grady also stated that it will be a seven to ten day trial, that there is an elder abuse claim and that they are requesting a jury on said claim. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Instruments, etc. is continued to December 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 in hopes to be in a position to set the trial. The Court directed the parties to submit a joint statement/status report indicating what is left to do and what the trial will look like by December 13, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Continued To Oct-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Continued To Sep-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for James Palmer (Respondent).

Counsel reported that they have completed the vast majority of discovery, that the remaining discovery should not be a problem, that they have been working together, that the interested parties are named in the petition and that the testamentary documents are not attached to the petition. The Court informed Counsel that the matter can not proceed to trial until the procedural issues are cleared up, that they need to amend the petition and noted that the Examiner's notes had been sent out a long, long time ago. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Instrument, etc. is continued to September 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Continued To Jul-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian O'Grady appeared by telephone for Delia Palma-Hauser (Petitioner).
John Kithas for James Palma (Respondent).

Counsel reported that they have completed quite a bit of discovery, that they still need to do some depositions, that they are still waiting for subpoenaed medical records, that they have not discussed mediation, and that they feel they can agree to do private mediation. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Instruments, etc. is continued to July 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 by which time discovery is to be completed and mediation is to be completed. If the matter is not settled, the Parties are to meet and confer on exactly how the case is to proceed towards settlement. A joint status statement is to be filed by July 18, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Instruments, To Impose Constructive Trust, For Financial Elder Abuse And To Set Aside Deed
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Continued To Apr-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Kithas for (respondent) James Palma. Brian J. O'Grady (via court call) for (petitioner) Delia Palma Hauser.

The Court stated that this is a petition to confirm the validity of trust instruments as well as elder abuse and undue influence and asked if the parties have talked about how to proceed. Mr. O'Grady stated that Mr. Kithas has prepared a proposed order to continue the matter to conduct discovery. Mr. Kithas stated that they took the deposition of a priest who knew the decedent and that they have a case management report due on April 18, 2017. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Instruments, etc., was continued to April 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the parties to meet and confer regarding what is left to do and what the issues are to be resolved and have engaged in settlement negotiations.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
